teams.name: Argentina
teams.summary: Argentina are the defending champions, having won the 2022 World Cup in Qatar on penalties against France. Led by Lionel Messi, they are ranked among the very best in the world and feature a strong mix of experience and emerging talent. They have won the World Cup three times (1978, 1986, 2022) and the Copa America in 2021 and 2024.

teams.name: France
teams.summary: France are perennial contenders and reached the 2022 final, losing to Argentina on penalties. They won the World Cup in 1998 on home soil and again in 2018 in Russia. With Kylian Mbappe leading the attack, France combine pace, power, and depth across every position.

teams.name: Brazil
teams.summary: Brazil are the most successful nation in World Cup history with five titles (1958, 1962, 1970, 1994, 2002). Known for their attacking flair, the current squad is built around Real Madrid forwards Vinicius Junior and Rodrygo. Brazil are always among the favourites at any World Cup.

teams.name: England
teams.summary: England won their only World Cup in 1966 on home soil. They have been consistent in recent tournaments, reaching the 2018 semi-final and back-to-back Euro finals in 2021 and 2024. A golden generation led by Harry Kane and Jude Bellingham makes them strong contenders for 2026.

teams.name: Spain
teams.summary: Spain won the World Cup in 2010 in South Africa and are the reigning European champions after winning Euro 2024. Their possession-based style is led by Ballon d'Or-calibre talents Rodri and teenage sensation Lamine Yamal.

teams.name: Germany
teams.summary: Germany have won the World Cup four times (1954, 1974, 1990, 2014). After disappointing group-stage exits in 2018 and 2022, a younger generation built around Jamal Musiala and Florian Wirtz is rebuilding the team's reputation.

teams.name: Portugal
teams.summary: Portugal have never won the World Cup, their best finish being third in 1966. Boasting a deep, talented squad and the legendary Cristiano Ronaldo, they won the UEFA Nations League and Euro 2016. Their golden generation makes them dark-horse contenders.

teams.name: Netherlands
teams.summary: The Netherlands have reached three World Cup finals (1974, 1978, 2010) but never won. Famous for 'Total Football', the current side is anchored by captain Virgil van Dijk and is always a tough, organised opponent.

teams.name: Belgium
teams.summary: Belgium's 'golden generation' achieved a third-place finish at the 2018 World Cup, their best ever result. Led by playmaker Kevin De Bruyne, they remain a dangerous attacking side transitioning toward younger talent.

teams.name: United States
teams.summary: The United States are co-hosts of the 2026 World Cup alongside Canada and Mexico. Their best finish was third place in the inaugural 1930 tournament. A young European-based core led by Christian Pulisic and coached by Mauricio Pochettino aims to make a deep run on home soil.

teams.name: Canada
teams.summary: Canada are co-hosts of the 2026 World Cup. After a 36-year absence, they qualified for Qatar 2022. Led by Bayern Munich's Alphonso Davies and striker Jonathan David, they are an exciting, rapidly improving side.

teams.name: Mexico
teams.summary: Mexico are co-hosts of the 2026 World Cup, becoming the first nation to host the tournament three times (1970, 1986, 2026). Perennial qualifiers, they have twice reached the World Cup quarter-finals (1970, 1986).
